quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: Hummingbirds are the only birds that can fly backwards! They can also hover in place by flapping their wings in a figure-eight pattern up to 80 times per second. The Pacific Northwest is home to several species, including the beautiful Rufous hummingbird that visits Port Angeles each summer.

short_read.title: The Secret Language of Trees
short_read.teaser: Scientists have discovered that trees communicate with each other through an underground network of fungi, sharing nutrients and warning each other of danger. This 'wood wide web' shows us that forests are more connected than we ever imagined.

medium_read.title: The Lost Art of Hungarian Embroidery
medium_read.teaser: For centuries, Hungarian women have created stunning embroidery patterns that tell stories of their villages and traditions. Today, a new generation is rediscovering these beautiful folk art techniques and keeping the heritage alive.

long_read.title: The Amazing Journey of Gray Whales Along the Pacific Coast
long_read.teaser: Every year, gray whales make one of the longest migrations of any mammal, traveling from Alaska to Mexico and back—right past Port Angeles. This is the story of their incredible journey, their near extinction, and their remarkable comeback.

port_angeles.title: Black-Tailed Deer at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: June is a wonderful time to spot black-tailed deer fawns at Hurricane Ridge! The meadows are blooming with wildflowers, and mother deer often bring their spotted babies out to graze in the early morning and evening. The little ones are usually born in late May and early June.

puzzle_1.question: A ruby-throated hummingbird's heart beats about ___ times per minute while hovering.
puzzle_1.correct_response: Exactly right! That tiny heart works incredibly hard to power those amazing wings!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! The answer is 1,200 beats per minute—that's about 20 times per second!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: It's an astonishing 1,200 beats per minute! Their hearts are true marvels of nature.
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: To fuel this incredible heart rate, hummingbirds must eat about half their body weight in nectar every day. They visit hundreds of flowers daily and can remember every flower they've been to!

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'butterfly'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Wonderful! Pillangó is such a lovely, musical word for these delicate creatures!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The Hungarian word for butterfly is 'pillangó'—doesn't it sound beautiful? (Madár means bird, virág means flower, and méhecske means little bee.)
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is filled with charming diminutives and poetic words for nature. The language creates many words by combining sounds that mirror what they describe, making it especially expressive.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree produces the largest cones of any conifer?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Yes! Sugar pine cones can grow up to 20 inches long—longer than your forearm! John Muir called them 'the king of all pines.'
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's the sugar pine! Their cones can reach 20 inches long and are truly spectacular. While they're more common in California, you can find some in southern Oregon.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: Sugar pines are the tallest pine trees in the world, reaching over 200 feet. Native Americans traditionally harvested their sweet, edible seeds, which taste a bit like pine nuts.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
